original_abstract: We present the Stanford CoreNLP toolkit, an integrated framework designed to provide a comprehensive suite of linguistic analysis tools for English. The pipeline offers a robust set of natural language processing annotations, including tokenization, sentence splitting, part-of-speech tagging, morphological analysis, named entity recognition, syntactic parsing (both constituency and dependency-based), and coreference resolution. Developed to be both flexible and efficient, CoreNLP allows users to easily apply a wide array of text analysis technology to large corpora. The system is designed with a focus on architectural consistency, providing a simplified API that enables researchers and practitioners to extract high-quality linguistic features with minimal configuration. Since its release, the toolkit has become a foundational resource in both academia and industry, supported by a modular design that facilitates the integration of new components and languages.
humanized_abstract: This paper describes the Stanford CoreNLP toolkit, a Java annotated library that provides a suite of applications for linguistic analysis of the English language. The applications in the pipeline perform a variety of tasks including tokenization and sentences splitting, part-of-speech tagging, fully automatic tagging, named entity recognition, parsing (constituent and dependency), and coreference resolution. CoreNLP is a very flexible and efficient system, allowing users to easily apply a large number of text analysis technologies to large corpora. The system was designed to be consistent in architecture and to have a simple API for extracting linguistic features from text in an easy way. Currently, the toolkit has become a widely used tool in both research and practice, which is supported by the modular design that allows easy extension with new applications and for processing of other languages.
